model_input:
You are a UI clicking agent. Given a screenshot of a screen, determine the pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at(x, y, confidence) with the center pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on an element in the provided image.
The image contains six buttons with the text "CLICK".
The buttons are arranged in two rows and three columns.
- Top row: Green, Green, Red
- Bottom row: Blue, Green, Yellow

The user didn't specify which "CLICK" button to click. I'll assume the first one (top-left) unless there's a standard convention.
Wait, usually when a user says "Call click_at()", they are asking me to perform the action of clicking.
Let's look for a target. There is no specific target mentioned.
Usually, in these benchmarks, if no target is specified, it might be a test of coordinate finding for a specific element.
However, I must provide a coordinate.

All buttons have a white border.

If I have to click one, I will click the top-left one.
Actually, I'll try to find if there is any other hint.
None.
